title: Pressure to Stick to Traditional Gender Roles: A Focus on Women Entrepreneurs
author: Debajyoti Sarkar
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0

steps.instruction: Introduction: The purpose of this research is to analyse the force to adhere to traditional gender roles on women entrepreneurs. Entrepreneurship is defined as an individual who arranges a business assuming economic stakes in the expectancy of proceeds and profits. Literature review: In this research researcher objectives and questions are developed. However, Entrepreneurship is the biggest challenge for women entrepreneurs nowadays. Methodology: In this research article, the researcher has selected the primary quantitative data collection method, which is followed by a survey. The researcher has made 15 close-ended questions according to the research topic. The researcher has gathered 65 responses from this survey. Findings: As the research article has selected the primary quantitative data collection method, therefore, data analysis technique has been obeyed by SPSS analysis. Discussion: Based on the literature review, research questions, primary quantitative data collection method, and SPSS analysis, the researcher has concluded the discussion as well as the conclusion. Conclusion: Like other developing countries, Indian women have businesses that are more infrequent or conduct business at a lower level.
